dna.borrowedTrait: two-column alternating-row rhythm for service entries with a thin vertical rule separating index number from descriptor
dna.divergenceRationale: All three recent own-builds used either centered-classic or warm-editorial archetypes with split-image or centered hero variants — magazine-split with an asymmetric-left hero is a deliberate structural departure that stays within the editorial/bold family appropriate for an implant-and-cosmetic specialist. Warm-editorial was explicitly ruled out because it telegraphs a community family practice personality that would undercut Dr. Etemadieh's specialist-premium positioning.
dna.creativeDirection: Cool forest-green authority anchored in a magazine-style split grid — the left column carries editorial type at unhurried scale, the right column holds the hero image in a contained frame with a slight bottom-left caption treatment, producing the sense of a specialist's portfolio rather than a clinical brochure. Every section breathes through generous white space and thin spatial dividers, letting the deep #2e6b3e palette surface as a confident accent rather than a wallpaper.
dna.brandSummary: Orange County Dental Care, led by Dr. Ali-Reza Etemadieh, serving Orange, — Cool forest-green authority anchored in a magazine-style split grid — the left column carries editorial type at unhurried scale, the right column holds the hero image in a contained frame with a slight bottom-left caption treatment, producing the sense of a specialist's portfolio rather than a clinical brochure. Every section breathes through generous white space and thin spatial dividers, letting the deep #2e6b3e palette surface as a confident accent rather than a wallpaper.
doRules: Use the archetype's established magazine-split layout — don't mix incompatible grid styles mid-page.
Hero variant must be asymmetric-left — lock this choice.
Card treatment is soft-shadow — apply consistently across all card instances.
Phone number must be a tel: link in the mobile header, minimum 44px touch target.
Doctor name and credentials must appear without scrolling on the homepage.
Primary CTA copy must reference a specific action and ideally the doctor or city.
dontRules: No gradient text (background-clip: text) — AI-slop tell #1.
No faux-glass / backdrop-filter cards unless archetype explicitly calls for it.
No centered-headline + two-CTA + microlinks hero — the most overused AI pattern.
No stat-bar section if yearsExperience/happyPatients data is missing — render empty = fail.
No stock photography of smiling model families.

No generic, boilerplate, or template-sounding copy. Every headline, tagline, and CTA must reference this specific practice, doctor, or city — never industry-generic phrasing that could appear on any competitor site.
No archetype+hero combo already used in recent own-builds.
No section that renders with dashes, nulls, or placeholder text visible to users.
dna.evalRationale: Candidate 2 edges ahead with the most specific and evocative creative direction — the caption treatment detail, 'contained frame,' and 'confident accent rather than wallpaper' palette description signal a genuinely considered visual system rather than a generic editorial template. Its section order also places reviews before gallery, creating a more natural trust-building narrative flow given that reviews are available and gallery is a supporting asset rather than the lead proof point.
